data.title: Acumatica reveals AI-enhancements for cloud ERP at Summit 2026, aiming to drive better decisions and outcomes for businesses.
data.description: In Bellevue, Washington, Acumatica presented its vision for the future of cloud ERP and introduced a range of AI-driven advancements during the Summit Day 2 Keynote. These innovations aim to assist organizations in enhancing agility, navigating complexities, improving decision-making, and achieving business success. The latest product upgrades highlight Acumatica's dedication to providing tangible innovation that yields tangible outcomes for expanding businesses through the integration of AI technology.

data.title: ComEd provides reliable power for 9M in Illinois, offers $500M in rebates for clean energy, EVs, and efficiency in 2025.
data.description: In 2025, ComEd made significant progress in meeting the energy demands of more than 9 million individuals in northern Illinois. The company prioritized enhancing electric utility reliability and successfully linked tens of thousands of clients to sustainable energy sources. They distributed over $500 million in rebates and energy efficiency initiatives to facilitate the adoption of electric vehicles, solar panels, battery storage systems, heat pumps, and other energy-saving technologies.

data.title: Millrock Technology appoints Tom Hochuli as CEO for freeze-drying & lyophilization solutions provider.
data.description: In Kingston, New York, on January 27, 2026, Millrock Technology Inc, a company specializing in freeze-drying and lyophilization solutions for the life sciences and biopharmaceutical sectors, revealed the selection of Tom Hochuli as its new Chief Executive Officer. Hochuli's appointment signifies a significant step for Millrock as he joins the team to lead the company forward in its mission.

data.title: Kase expands East Coast fulfillment with new facility in Northampton, Pennsylvania, enhancing nationwide reach and omnichannel capabilities.
data.description: A leading logistics provider, Kase, recently unveiled its latest fulfillment center in Northampton, Pennsylvania on Jan. 27, 2026. This strategic location on the East Coast is aimed at enhancing nationwide reach and optimizing omnichannel operations. This facility is set to bolster Kase's distribution network, ensuring efficient delivery services across the country.

data.title: Schouw & Co. reports good 2025 results, with revenue of approx DKK 34.1 billion and EBITDA around DKK 2,875 million. Strong outlook for 2026.
data.description: According to the initial and unaudited financial data of the Group’s subsidiaries, Schouw & Co. is now projected to generate a revenue of approximately DKK 34.1 billion in 2025, as opposed to the previous estimate of DKK 33.7-34.7 billion. The anticipated EBITDA for the Group is around DKK 2,875 million, which falls within the earlier projected range of DKK 2,850-3,020 million.

data.title: Cadonix introduces smartBuild at WHMA Summit in Las Vegas, enhancing wire harness production with margins up by 2-6% and production speed up to 40%.
data.description: An innovative technology called smartBuild was unveiled at the WHMA Annual Global Leadership Summit held in Las Vegas. This cutting-edge system offers guided digital assembly, at-station testing, and cloud traceability, resulting in a noticeable increase in profit margins by 2-6% per harness. Additionally, production speed is boosted by up to 40%. The launch took place in Framingham, Mass., on January 27, 2026.

data.title: IMLS increases funding for projects countering anti-Semitism; 4 institutions to receive support.
data.description: Four organizations have been selected to receive funding for initiatives aimed at combating hate. The Institute for Museum and Library Services (IMLS) made this announcement on January 27, 2026, marking International Holocaust Remembrance Day. This initiative reflects the commitment to honoring the memory of the Holocaust and promoting tolerance.

data.title: Airspan partners with Space Compass for 5G connectivity in high-altitude maritime surveillance, from 16-18 km altitude.
data.description: A company based in Plano, Texas, Airspan Networks Holdings LLC, has revealed its intention to implement a 5G Air-to-Ground communications setup for Space Compass's High-Altitude Platform Station. This initiative aims to facilitate a fresh era of Maritime Domain Awareness via the stratosphere. By leveraging 5G connectivity at an altitude of 16-18 km, the project will showcase the ability to ensure secure command-and-control, as well as the real-time transmission of mission data.
U.S. GOVERNMENT TO SOON ISSUE GENERAL LICENSE LIFTING SOME SANCTIONS ON VENEZUELAN OIL INDUSTRY, SOURCES SAY.

data.title: ACMG announces 2026 Medical Genetics Awareness Week on March 10-14, sponsored by MyOme, celebrating professionals.
data.description: In Bethesda, Maryland, on January 27, 2026, the American College of Medical Genetics and Genomics (ACMG) revealed plans for the eighth installment of Medical Genetics Awareness Week. Sponsored by MyOme, the event is scheduled for March 10-14, 2026. This annual week aims to engage the public, health professionals, patients, and other stakeholders in discussions and activities centered around medical genetics.

data.title: Dealerware introduces new insurance verification features for automotive retailers, improving check-in experiences. Texas-based company aims for efficiency and simplicity. (Char. count: 139)
data.description: In a recent announcement on January 27, 2026, in Austin, Texas, Dealerware, a prominent fleet management and mobility platform for automotive retailers, introduced new improvements aimed at aiding in handling insurance complications. These enhancements are designed to ensure that automotive retailers can efficiently manage insurance complexities without compromising the speed and flexibility of their check-in processes. This update is set to provide a more streamlined experience for both the retailers and their customers, ultimately improving their operational efficiency.

data.title: Chartwells Higher Education's Campus Dining Index reports high-protein meals as top choice for college students in 2026, emphasizing cleaner ingredients and functional beverages.
data.description: In the year 2026, the Campus Dining Index conducted one of the largest annual surveys focusing on dietary trends and dining program preferences across the country. According to the survey results, high-protein meals emerged as the most favored dining choice among college students. The study also revealed a growing interest in clean and minimally processed foods. These findings shed light on the current culinary inclinations of students, emphasizing a preference for protein-rich dishes.

data.title: All County Central Property Management in Birmingham shows rapid growth & strong leasing with owner Terrence Henry.
data.description: In Birmingham, Alabama on January 27, 2026, the All County Property Management Franchise Corp. announced the impressive growth and leasing accomplishments of Terrence Henry, the owner of All County Central Property Management. Henry has demonstrated his expertise in swiftly filling vacancies and providing successful leasing services.

data.title: Gas thyristor market expanding globally, size reached USD 1,521.77 million in 2024, projected to hit USD 2,172.33 billion by 2032.
data.description: As of January 27, 2026, the worldwide thyristor market is experiencing steady growth in power and industrial sectors. In 2018, the market was estimated at USD 1,350.00 million, climbing to USD 1,521.77 million in revenue by 2024. Projections indicate that the market value is expected to hit USD 2,172.33 million by the near future.

data.title: Sparkle Grooming Co. grants 3 franchise licenses to entrepreneur Wendi Wolmer in Chicago, expanding wellness pet care.
data.description: Local entrepreneur Wendi Wolmer has been awarded three salon franchises in Chicago by Sparkle Grooming Co., a rapidly-growing dog grooming franchise. The wellness-focused pet care salons are set to introduce new services to the city. The announcement was made in Scottsdale, Arizona on January 27, 2026. These franchise licenses mark a significant expansion for Sparkle Grooming Co. as they continue to establish their presence in the pet care industry.
